hardware.gpu: Intel Arc 140V (Xe2) — NOT used for this measurement; encryption is CPU-side only

software.aes_acceleration: AES-NI available on Lunar Lake (hardware-accelerated AES-256-GCM)

methodology.fixture: ~107 chunks synthetic substrate (50 doc chunks, 57 turns), created with fake bag-of-words embedder to avoid GPU model load cost
not_measured: GPU/OpenVINO inference (not on this path — encryption is CPU-side AES-GCM, not GPU)
Real bge-small-en-v1.5 ONNX embedder load (fake embedder used to isolate crypto overhead)
Disk I/O for DB open (measured from object construction, includes SQLite open + schema check + cache decrypt)
Memory footprint of the embedding cache (107 * 384 * 4 bytes = ~164 KB)

pre_encryption_baseline_retrieval_ms.description: SubstrateStore (plaintext) retrieve() latency, same fixture, same queries

boot_cache_decrypt_ms.description: One-time cost at store construction: decrypt all 107 embeddings from disk into in-RAM cache
boot_cache_decrypt_ms.note: Paid ONCE at unlock; subsequent queries operate on plaintext in-memory vectors

per_query_retrieval_ms.description: EncryptedSubstrateStore retrieve() latency: embed query + cosine over in-RAM cache + decrypt top-k text

delta_vs_baseline.note: ~0.1ms per-query overhead from top-k text decrypt; boot-cache decrypt is one-time at unlock

interpretation.boot_overhead: 1.45ms one-time at unlock for 107 embeddings. Scales linearly — 10k embeddings ~130ms, still sub-second.
interpretation.per_query_overhead: ~0.1ms per query (top-k text decrypt). Immaterial vs LLM TTFT (~940ms measured on this hardware).
interpretation.embedding_cache_design: Encrypting embeddings at rest (closing the vec2text semantic-shadow leak) costs nothing at query time because decryption is a single boot-time pass. The encrypted-at-rest + plaintext-in-RAM design gives full at-rest protection at negligible runtime cost.
